response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer whose career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. His C++ exposure is limited to a single 6-month role at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project, far short of the deep, continuous C++/STL systems-programming expertise this role demands. He has no demonstrated experience with industrial communication protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), or on-premises industrial automation platforms, making him a poor fit for the core technical and domain requirements of this position.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Active mentorship culture: organized recurring Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and multi-day training courses
Broad polyglot background spanning embedded, web, mobile, and IoT-adjacent (GISCollective) domains shows adaptability
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professional, one thesis project) — the role requires deep, continuous C++/STL expertise as its primary competency
No exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0) or IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle
Entire career trajectory is web/cloud-native (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS); no industrial automation, SCADA, or embedded systems platform experience
